operating_state: Total revenue increased 6.5% to $55.3 billion driven by 5% comparable sales growth from higher shopping frequency and average ticket, plus 20 net new warehouses. Core merchandise sales rose 6% led by foods and sundries while non-foods declined. Membership fee revenue grew 6% to $1.0 billion from new sign-ups, Executive upgrades, and improved renewal rates reaching 92.6%. Warehouse ancillary businesses grew 9% with gasoline volume increases contributing positively.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage improved 8 basis points to 10.72% primarily from favorable comparison to prior year LIFO charges, partially offset by pressure from core merchandise categories in non-foods and fresh foods. Merchandise costs continued experiencing inflation impacts with management employing supplier cost-sharing, volume purchasing, and selective price adjustments. SG&A expenses as percentage of sales increased 13 basis points to 9.11% primarily from central operating costs including multi-year tax audit charges.
material_events: Board authorized new $4.0 billion share repurchase program expiring January 2027, replacing prior authorization. Company repurchased 579,000 shares for $285 million during first half. Quarterly dividend increased to $0.90 per share from $0.79 prior year. Company opened 12 new warehouses including two relocations with 15 additional planned for remainder of fiscal year.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation negatively impacted net sales by $937 million and gross margin by $91 million, primarily from Canadian and Other International operations. Interest income increased significantly to $105 million from $6 million in prior year due to higher global interest rates. Operations continue facing merchandise cost inflation across categories with tariff exposure remaining dependent on goods type and timing.
forward_outlook: Management plans capital expenditures of $3.8-4.2 billion for fiscal 2023, financing through operating cash flows and existing liquidity. Capital allocation continues emphasizing new warehouse expansion with higher growth rates expected internationally due to smaller base. E-commerce operations expected to continue with lower gross margins than warehouse operations.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple California labor law class actions and PAGA claims pending regarding seating, wage payments, and working conditions. Opioid litigation consolidated in federal MDL includes various claims against the Company. Capital expenditure plans subject to change based on economic environment. Company maintains various bank credit facilities with most expiring within one year.
